title: 10 best herbal teas for acid reflux

remedy_properties: Iridoid glycosides: Yellow gentian tea contains iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to relax the esophageal sphincter and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, helping to alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in yellow gentian tea have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining, thereby reducing acid reflux symptoms.
Tannins: Tannins are compounds found in the tea that can help bind to excess stomach acid, reducing the amount of acid available to flow back up into the esophagus and cause discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gentiana lutea, also known as yellow gentian, teas helps with acid reflux because of its unique ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut health. The root of the plant contains compounds like gentiopicrin, which have been shown to enhance the production of digestive juices and accelerate gastric emptying. By increasing the efficiency of digestion and reducing the likelihood of stomach contents backing up into the esophagus, Gentiana lutea teas can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms. This natural remedy promotes a balanced digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Gentianine: It is an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and prevent acid reflux.
Gentianic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect the esophagus from acid damage and reduce the severity of acid reflux symptoms.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain bioactive compounds that help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: They possess anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing the digestive system and alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
Flowers: They have a calming effect on the digestive system and help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 dried gentiana lutea roots, weighing about 20-30 grams, for a single serving of tea.
Chop the gentiana lutea roots into smaller pieces using a sharp knife for easier steeping.
Combine 5 grams of chopped gentiana lutea roots with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container before serving.

remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tea's carminative properties help to reduce gas and bloating, which can contribute to acid reflux by relaxing the muscles in the esophageal sphincter and reducing inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, teas helps with acid reflux because its active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, have a natural relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract. remedy_constituents: Anethole: This terpene helps with acid reflux by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms that can contribute to heartburn and indigestion.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Fruits: Employed for their calming and carminative properties, which aid in reducing stomach acid and alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh foeniculum vulgare stems and leaves for optimal flavor and aroma release.
Gently clean the harvested foeniculum vulgare with lukewarm water to remove dirt and impurities.
Chop the cleaned foeniculum vulgare into small pieces to enhance infusion efficiency and brewing time.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of foeniculum vulgare in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to create a clear and fragrant herbal tea drink.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and leaves from a clean and pest-free environment.

Add 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse fully.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 inches of fresh roots in the morning to maximize flavor and aroma potential.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with acid reflux because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and saponins that work together to soothe the digestive tract. The flavonoids calm the inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux. Meanwhile, the terpenes relax the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acids from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, saponins help to reduce the production of stomach acid, further alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, present in purple coneflower tea have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Tannins: Tannins in purple coneflower tea help to tighten the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reducing symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid, found in purple conefflower tea have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a good source of salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Buds: The buds contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making them effective in soothing the digestive system and reducing acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers for the tea.
Measure and prepare the ingredients according to the required dosages.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow the herbal properties to release.
Strain and serve the tea in a warm temperature to facilitate effective absorption.
Drink the tea after meals and as needed to help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with acid reflux because of its unique composition that soothes and calms the digestive tract. The mucilage content in comfrey acts as a natural barrier, coating the esophageal lining and reducing inflammation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, comfrey's anti-inflammatory properties help to neutralize stomach acids, preventing them from flowing back up into the esophagus. By promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria, comfrey also helps to regulate digestion, further alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Comfrey tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and heal the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
Allantoin: Allantoin, a compound found in comfrey, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms.
Antacid effects: Comfrey tea has been shown to have antacid properties, helping to neutralize excess stomach acid and relieve heartburn and indigestion symptoms associated with acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of comfrey contain a high concentration of allantoin, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach.
Rhizomes: Comfrey rhizomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried symphytum officinale leaves and flowers in bulk quantities for future use.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried herb for every eight ounces of boiling water required for steeping.
Bring the measured water to a boil then remove from heat allowing it to simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to catch any loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before consumption within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry teas helps with acid reflux because of its natural antacid properties, which help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The antioxidants present in blueberries also help to soothe and protect the lining of the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds found in herbal blueberry teas may help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and relieving symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in blueberry tea, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by neutralizing free radicals.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in blueberry tea, including quercetin and kaempferol, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to acid reflux symptoms.

Soften-and-solubilize mucilage properties: Blueberry tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the esophageal mucosa, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, blueberry roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, including acid reflux.
Fruits (specifically, the ripe fruit pulp): The sweet and slightly tart flavor of ripe blueberries helps neutralize stomach acidity and coat the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air-drying method.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried vaccinium myrtillus leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herb mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow active compounds to release.
Strain and discard solids, then sweeten with honey if desired and enjoy hot or cold tea within 24 hours.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acid reflux that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbs like peppermint, ginger, and fennel have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. Other herbal teas used to treat these conditions include chamomile, lemon balm, and licorice root tea, which can also calm the digestive system and reduce discomfort.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for acid reflux are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and enhancing digestion. The best herbal teas for acid reflux include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Althaea officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Camellia sinensis, Aloe ferox, and Rauvolfia serpentina. If left untreated, acid reflux can lead to digestive discomfort, sleep disturbances, and social isolation, negatively impacting one's quality of life, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with acid reflux is a blend of ginger, peppermint, and licorice root. Ginger tea helps to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort, while peppermint tea calms the stomach and reduces symptoms of heartburn. Licorice root tea soothes the esophagus and reduces acidity. Drinking these teas individually or in combination can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms. A suggested ratio is 2 parts ginger to 1 part peppermint to 1 part licorice root.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), which included a comprehensive review of 22 studies on the effects of medicinal plants on gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), the researchers searched Medline, Scopus, and Cochrane Central Register of Controlled Trials for data from January 1966 to October 2015. The study found that all herbal preparations used in human studies led to the alleviation of symptoms related to GERD, with some, such as Myrtus communis and Cydonia oblonga, showing a marked reduction in GERD symptoms comparable to the proton pump inhibitor omeprazole. Furthermore, the therapeutic effects of these herbal preparations, including Cydonia oblonga, persisted after discontinuation of the drug, suggesting their potential as a long-term solution for managing GERD symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with acid reflux because it contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophageal lining and reduce inflammation. This anti-inflammatory effect also helps to decrease the production of stomach acid, thereby reducing acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, the tea's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the stomach lining, preventing acid from coming into contact with the sensitive tissues and reducing discomfort. This dual action provides relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with acid reflux by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and preventing the relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter, thereby preventing acid reflux.
Saponins: They help with acid reflux by forming a protective barrier in the stomach and esophagus, reducing the irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps with acid reflux by inhibiting the production of stomach acid, reducing inflammation in the esophagus, and promoting the healing of esophageal mucosa.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for acid reflux because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for acid reflux because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.

Fruits: The fruits of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for acid reflux because they contain glycosides and other compounds that have a soothing effect on the digestive system and help alleviate heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra and place it in a tea infuser.
Boil 8 ounces of water for 3 to 5 minutes and bring it to a rolling boil.
Steep the dried root in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to allow flavors to release.
Strain the tea and discard the root solids to obtain a clear herbal tea.
Allow the tea to cool for 2 to 5 minutes before serving and enjoying its sweet flavor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with acid reflux because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to slow down gastric emptying, allowing for better digestion and reducing the likelihood of stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. This natural remedy promotes a healthy digestive balance.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: They help with acid reflux by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and relaxing the muscles in the stomach, thereby preventing the backflow of stomach acid.
Shogaols: They help with acid reflux by inhibiting the production of gastric acid and reducing the contractions of the stomach muscles that can contribute to acid reflux.

Volatile oils (specifically, the sesquiterpenes): They help with acid reflux by reducing inflammation and relaxing the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the ginger plant are commonly used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are often used in teas for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the digestive system and reduce acid reflux.

Leaves: While less commonly used than the rhyzomes or roots, the leaves of the ginger plant may still be used in teas due to their mild, anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate mild acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of fresh or dried zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water for infusion.
Rinse the root in cold water to remove any impurities for 2-3 minutes.
Chop the root into small pieces to release its active compounds for 5 minutes.
Steep the chopped root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the infusion through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The active compounds in Foeniculum vulgare, such as anethole and limonene, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and relax the muscles in the stomach, preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare teas have been found to increase the production of stomach mucus, which helps to protect the esophagus from acid damage and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Acts as an antispasmodic agent, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce the severity of acid reflux symptoms.
Foeniculin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Fenchone: Demonstrates antacid and antispasmodic properties, helping to neutralize stomach acid and reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract that can contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Foeniculum vulgare to make teas for acid reflux due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which helps soothe digestive issues.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are another key component, as they are rich in anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that aid in relieving acid reflux symptoms.

Stems: Foeniculum vulgare stems also contain anethole and other volatile oils, making them a valuable part in teas for acid reflux due to their ability to calm digestive issues and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems from the plant during the summer months.
Clean the harvested foeniculum vulgare thoroughly by rinsing with cold water for 2 minutes.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the cleaned foeniculum vulgare with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reaches a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve and serve immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas helps with acid reflux because of its unique combination of natural compounds. The tea contains quinine, which has been shown to have a direct effect on the stomach muscles, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, the tea's alkaline properties help to neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues, making it a popular alternative to traditional acid reflux treatments.
remedy_constituents: Quinic acid: Helps to reduce the acidity of the stomach and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by neutralizing excess hydrochloric acid.
Quinovic acid: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, reducing inflammation in the esophagus and protecting against damage caused by acid reflux.
Quinidine: Exhibits antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis are primarily used because of their high content of alkaloids, specifically quinine, which helps in soothing heartburn and acid reflux.
Barks: The barks are used due to their rich content of alkaloids, including quinine and cinchonine, which have anti-inflammatory and acid-reducing properties.
Stems: The stems of Cinchona officinalis are used in teas for acid reflux because they contain a significant amount of alkaloids, which help in reducing inflammation and neutralizing stomach acid.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh cinchona officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves from a trusted source.
Rinse the harvested cinchona officinalis leaves with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves and any solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas helps with acid reflux because its main active compound, menthol, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophageal lining and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, mentha piperita's calming effects on the digestive system help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. This natural remedy also has a mild antacid effect, further alleviating acid reflux symptoms. As a result, mentha piperita teas can provide quick relief from acid reflux discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has a calming effect on the stomach muscles, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to acid reflux.
Limonene: Limonene has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Roscavin: Roscavin has a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing acidity and inflammation in the stomach, which can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, are commonly used to make teas for acid reflux because they contain menthol and menthone, which help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used to make teas for acid reflux due to their high concentration of menthol and other bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system.

Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used to make teas for acid reflux because they contain similar bioactive compounds to the leaves and roots, which help ease digestion and reduce symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal flavor and aroma.
Rinse the harvested leaves and flowers with clean water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried mentha x piperita leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to separate the leaves from the liquid.
Serve the tea warm or chilled and enjoy as a refreshing and relaxing herbal infusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis teas helps with acid reflux because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The mucilages present in the plant form a protective barrier in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by stomach acid. By coating the esophagus and stomach, it prevents acid from coming into contact with these sensitive areas, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, Althaea officinalis teas has a calming effect on the digestive system, promoting relaxation and reducing spasms that can contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with acid reflux.
Polysaccharides: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms.
Phenolic acids: Have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help to reduce the production of stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in mucilage, a soothing substance that helps to protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing acid reflux symptoms.
Leaves: They contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis contain mucilage, similar to the roots, which can help to soothe and protect the digestive tract, providing relief from acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots in the fall after the plant has finished flowering and has gone dormant for winter.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to make the tea.
Strain the tea and drink immediately to enjoy the potential health benefits of althaea officinalis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The tea's calming effects on the nervous system also help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties may help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms. By promoting a balanced digestive system, Lavandula angustifolia tea may provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce stomach acid production and inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, soothing the esophageal lining and reducing irritation caused by acid reflux.
Caryophyllene oxide: Displays a relaxing effect on the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are commonly used to make teas because they contain calming essential oils that can reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues.
Buds: Lavandula angustifolia buds are used in teas for their potential to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers when they are in full bloom usually in mid to late summer.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a cool dark place for 1 to 2 weeks to preserve their aroma.
Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their essential oils and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis teas helps with acid reflux because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The catechins and other polyphenols present in the tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, the tea's alkaline nature helps balance the body's pH levels, neutralizing stomach acid and providing relief from heartburn and discomfort. This natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective way to manage acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds help with acid reflux by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of Helicobacter pylori bacteria, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms.
Quinic acid: Quinic acid helps with acid reflux by neutralizing stomach acid and reducing the production of gastric acid, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, which are formed during the fermentation process, help with acid reflux by reducing inflammation and improving the barrier function of the esophageal mucosa, preventing acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Camellia sinensis, as they contain the highest concentration of L-theanine, an amino acid that helps reduce stress and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Buds: Young tea buds are used to make green tea, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing acid reflux.

Stems: Some tea varieties use the stems of the Camellia sinensis plant, which contain a higher concentration of antioxidants than leaves, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves in the early morning after the dew has dried when they are at their freshest state.
Rinse 1 teaspoon of the leaves with filtered water to remove any impurities and then drain thoroughly.
Steep the leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 3 to 5 minutes to allow the flavors to release.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or a tea strainer to prevent any solids.
Serve the tea hot or iced depending on personal preference and enjoy the rich flavors and aromas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe ferox teas helps with acid reflux because its soothing properties calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Aloe ferox, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to neutralize stomach acid and promote a healthy gut environment. This results in a significant reduction in acid reflux symptoms, including heartburn and regurgitation, allowing for a more comfortable and peaceful digestive experience. Regular consumption of Aloe ferox teas can provide long-term relief.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and prevent acid reflux symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Aloe ferox teas have antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to acid reflux and inflammation.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Aloe ferox teas have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of aloe vera gel, which helps soothe and calm the digestive system.
Roots: The roots are used to make teas that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Stems: The stems are also used to make teas that help calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 aloe ferox leaves from mature plants with yellow or orange tips.
Wash the leaves with cold water to remove dirt and other contaminants thoroughly.
Remove the thick green skin from the leaves using a sharp knife carefully.
Chop the flesh into small pieces and weigh out 5 grams for every 250 milliliters.
Steep the chopped aloe ferox in boiling water for 10 to 20 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas helps with acid reflux because of its alkalizing properties and ability to relax the lower esophageal sphincter. The plant's alkaloids, particularly serpentine and ajmaline, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, soothing heartburn and discomfort. Additionally, the tea's sedative properties calm the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress-induced acid reflux. As a result, Rauvolfia serpentina teas may provide relief from acid reflux symptoms, promoting a more comfortable digestive experience.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Help alleviate acid reflux by reducing the secretion of stomach acid and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, thus preventing the backflow of stomach contents into the esophagus.
Steroids: Act as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Glycosides: Help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux, and promoting a healthy balance of stomach acid and digestive enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain alkaloids like reserpine, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help in soothing acid reflux symptoms.
Roots: The roots are a rich source of alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, making them effective in treating acid reflux.
Barks: The barks of Rauvolfia serpentina contain alkaloids that have a calming effect on the stomach, reducing acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of fresh or dried root of this plant in early morning or evening.
Dry the harvested root in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature before drinking 2-3 times a day.
